title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. flaccida
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. flaccida, commonly known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its rosettes of toothed leaves and clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which make it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of lady's mantle include soothing digestive issues, reducing menstrual cramps, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, lady's mantle is often used as a tincture, tea, or cream, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
